stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 3 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 20 observations with mean 39.7 /10 (within-chain SD = 9.1). chain B has 10 observations with mean 34.3 /10 (within-chain SD = 8.8). chain C has 10 observations with mean 45.8 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.9). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.9 and 12.3 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain A: 39.7 /10 (shrunk from raw 39.7). Range [39.7, 39.800000000000004] as between-chain SD varies from 4.9 to 12.3. Pooled mean: 39.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 39.9 /10 (weighted average of 3 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=8.2: B = 0.942.
Interval [39.7, 39.800000000000004] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (39.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (39.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
